reviews.review_description:
If you are still stuck. Water the yellow flower then the flower bud floats in the attic. Pick up the game board when you see the pink bud less flower and connect it. The book teaches you.
Also love the game. Hope you guys are making a sequel.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for playing & for sharing these tips with others! We would love to make a sequel :)

reviews.review_description: So neat unlike any other game on quest!! Honestly stuck tho and been for a while I need to knw how u get the watering or dnt knw wtf to do w after I water the flower and then it’s floating?? How do u get floating flower to the  to helps vines grow!! Help plzzz Great job devs and plz wanna see mor from u all!! Very unique!👍🏼🥰   Link in profile follow for tons of codes will follow bk and play/check daily, and positive I hav something u probably want to get I’m a tad addicted!!!😆

developer_response.body:
Hello and thank you for playing! For help and tips, you can always join the Mighty Yell Discord - but another user also provided this hint:
If you are still stuck. Water the yellow flower then the flower bud floats in the attic. Pick up the game board when you see the pink bud less flower and connect it. The book teaches you.

reviews.review_description: This is my second time playing this game since I bought it over a year ago. I really like it but now it having freezing issues. And twice it just quit completely on me. I am at the very end of the game and cannot finish because each time I try to talk to the knight in the final battle, it freezes. I cannot respond to him with the A button. I have tried over and over but it freezes every time. I did not have this issue on my first time around in the game. I checked to see if it needed an update but it does not. Can the developer help me get through the game please.

developer_response.body: We are sorry to hear you're having this issue! As we can't recreate this, it sounds like it could require a reinstall. Apologies for an frustration, and feel free to reach out if this is not solving the issue yell [at] mightyyell [dot[ com

reviews.review_description: Too frustrating, just seeing a tiny portion of the "game board." Seems like a poor use of VR. The "out of control" rolling motion is frustrating too. I would love to be immersed in this land (at least on occasion) to add some dynamic and excitement. Hubby and I both feel this way.

Find a couple friends to act out the dialog and add more story. Otherwise its a very slow boring marble balance game with very little story.  Shame because It had potential. I thought is was going to be more like Table of Tales.

developer_response.body: We would love to add voice over in the future, thank you for taking the time to share your feedback!

reviews.review_description:
I didn't think there would be much to this at first but the price was irresistible. It does start out pretty simple but with a unique charm. I'm up to level 8 now and the puzzles are stepping up in cleverness. If they don't become very frustrating, I suspect I'll be left wanting more when I finish the game. 

Great to pick up even if you have limited time. The levels aren't too long. I should add I'm playing with the joystick which, as another reviewer said, doesn't detract from enjoying it (I'm not a huge fan of this marble mechanic to start with).

Like the title says, this is a delightful and relaxing little game, not particularly challenging but still quite a bit of fun. The graphics aren't amazing but they look better in VR than they do in the promotional video. I would recommend this to anyone who wants a chill little puzzler to enjoy.

I really like this game I think the design is really fun and clever and it has such a great art style! The characters are so cute and I really like the story so far! I’m really excited to go deeper into this game! 
I also like that you can sit down while you play this and the different options you have for controlling the character. 

If you like cute adventure/puzzle games this one is for you!

reviews.review_title: A surprise, and a fun one so far
reviews.review_description:
My first impressions weren’t good.

Initially the game had a slightly primitive presentation that made me fear that this would be yet another unsatisfying indie game with good ideas but without the technical prowess to execute them properly.

The premise of the game is very clever. You have to roll a little marble person through an adventure inside a magic toy that you discovered in your grandmother’s attic.

Unless something changes for the worse, it looks like the developers had the technical skills to pull it off.

A few minutes into the game I was smiling and having fun.  The graphics are quite pleasant once the game gets going.

I’ve seen only one minor technical issue so far. There is a brief slowdown when dropping a new level into the box.  The music also ends after a short while in each level, but I don’t know if this is intentional or a glitch. Either way it doesn’t bother me so I hesitate to call it a technical issue.

The basic “roll a marble through a maze by tilting the box” mechanic works very well and feels just like the real thing.

Some other object manipulations are a bit clunky, (I don’t want to say what they are to avoid spoilers), but after a little fiddling and practice they work fine.

This is more of an action game than a puzzle game which was a surprise. However it’s so enjoyable I found that to be a pleasant surprise. (There are some puzzles and they’re occurring more frequently, so the puzzle aspect may get more challenging over time.)

I’ll update my review after I’ve completed the game and adjust the score if necessary. I think it may end up warranting a five star review, especially considering its low price.

Its a very well done game with a totally unique concept that works great in VR. I love that you can play different styles and use the board with your hand like a normal marble board would do or use your thumbsticks for easier locomotion. Also there is some new mechanics in almost every level. Some of them made me laught because it was such a fun and differnt approach to the normal marbleing around. Only thing that would make it even better is a version that only uses handtracking.

developer_response.body: We love this idea; thank you so much for playing!

reviews.review_description:
This game took me by surprise. I was expecting an easy, fun, cute game like The Curious Tales of the Stolen Pets, but I got much more.

The game takes place in Camelot, where you play as Guinevere to save the kingdom. Here is where the game gets interesting and really fun.

You don't move the character around like you would in a normal platformer type game. Instead you roll them around the world like a marble. Think of one of those old marble boxes, where you had to roll the marble through a labyrinth without falling in any holes.

This really adds a fun challenge to a cozy game. The hand controls on the box feel amazing, it's like holding the real thing.

This is also a game you want to show your friends and stream to the TV. Everyone watching me play wanted a turn to jump in and see if they could face the rolling challenges.

Wasn't sure about this game although I thought it looked cute.   I went a head and got it. So glad I did. What a hidden gem.   I have only played about 30 minutes so don't know how long the game last but I had to write a review. Very impressed!Very different game play. Graphics are nice and smooth. Controls are great.  Very nice get lost in game and puzzle. Well done!  Can't wait to get back to it!!
Great little story, fun interactions. Different than what I expected, but enjoyed.

reviews.review_title: Innovative mechanics and a fun story
reviews.review_description: This game is a really cool experience, especially if you played with little marlble labyrinths as a kid. You travel through the world using similar mechanics to the old fashioned toy, but interact with the world in a way that reminded me a bit of moss… fairly basic story based on King Arthur and Guinevere but fits the gameplay well and serves its purpose of giving you reasons to travel through the world. Not quite a AAA experience but for the price you really can’t beat this title!
I love the table top experience. This gives you a visually stunning puzzling table top adventure. Excellent work developers

I really enjoyed this game because it was so different ( and the $6.99 sale price was too good to pass up).  The visuals were good and I definitely had to think some things through. I do wish there was a full walkthrough on the web. When I couldn’t solve level 9 I contacted customer service who sent me a video clip. 

I would also suggest that you can pick up where you left and not have to start the whole level again.

This one truly took me by surprise. I’ve seen this game in the store here and there when it’s on Sale, but it never really grabbed me. A buddy decided to gift it to me, and my god, I’m so happy he did!

After reaching level 4 I decided to check the price, figuring it likely cost between $15-$20. I was pretty stunned it only cost $8… Not only reasonable, it’s a STEAL!  I really hope there’s DLC being developed or even a full blown sequel. I’ll happily pay the admission 
fee. “TAKE MY MONEY!!”

I went into this thinking it might be just another maze game, but for $10 I would gladly try it. After about 10 minutes I was pleasantly surprised and thoroughly hooked. The only reason I didn’t finish the game in one sitting is because I really need to get some sleep. This game is absolutely worth checking out

reviews.review_title: Cute litte game with good ideas
reviews.review_description:
Cute little game with great ideas for interacting with the game board and the character Guinevere. Controlling the character works great. At the beginning, it's fun and interesting to control the game board by tilting and holding it, but I quickly switched to using the joystick - which doesn't detract from the fun. The puzzles start off easy and become more complex as the story and chapters progress, but they are never really difficult and rarely challenging, but - and this is very good - never frustrating or unfair. The execution is charming. The little world is beautifully designed, the ideas for interaction fit very well in VR and are well implemented.

I felt entertained throughout the entire game. However, there is still the feeling that more could be done in terms of puzzle depth, story arc, music, combat, traps, (hidden) collectibles, etc.

For puzzle enthusiasts and the low price, in my opinion, it is worth picking up without hesitation.

Gear doesn’t show up properly to use and get past level/spot


why can’t you actually start where you leave off? You have all the blue placemarker spots that it shoots you back to if you run into something and mess up it puts you back to but if you stop the game and are not at a full game break chapter, you have to REDO THE WHOLE LEVEL!
